title: Handle a full bag in a traditional small sort system
preconditions: Operator is assigned sorter destinations
A parcel bag at a destination has become full
procedure_visual_summary: Text slide comparing traditional and Fortna solutions; no UI, but includes explicit traditional full-bag handling sequence.

steps.instruction: Close the destination when the bag becomes full.
steps.expected_outcome: The full destination is closed.
steps.validation_check: Destination has been closed for the full bag condition.
steps.visual_region_hint: Bullet describing the traditional small sort system process.

steps.instruction: Print a label.
steps.expected_outcome: A label is available for the full bag.
steps.validation_check: Label has been printed.
steps.visual_region_hint: Bullet describing the traditional small sort system process.

steps.instruction: Apply the label to the bag.
steps.expected_outcome: The bag is labeled.
steps.validation_check: Printed label is attached to the bag.
steps.visual_region_hint: Bullet describing the traditional small sort system process.

steps.instruction: Zip up the bag.
steps.expected_outcome: The full bag is closed.
steps.validation_check: Bag is zipped.
steps.visual_region_hint: Bullet describing the traditional small sort system process.

steps.instruction: Place the bag on a takeaway belt.
steps.expected_outcome: The bag is transferred to takeaway.
steps.validation_check: Bag is on the takeaway belt.
steps.visual_region_hint: Bullet describing the traditional small sort system process.

steps.instruction: Reopen the chute.
steps.expected_outcome: The chute is reopened for continued parcel flow.
steps.validation_check: Chute has been reopened after bag removal.
steps.visual_region_hint: Bullet describing the traditional small sort system process.

retrieval_text: Traditional full-bag handling procedure: when the bag becomes full, the operator closes the destination, prints a label, applies the label to the bag, zips the bag, places it on a takeaway belt, and reopens the chute.

title: Mark chute unavailable when a tote is full
preconditions: A tote has reached the full threshold.
procedure_visual_summary: Slide describes parcel sorting into a tote, tote fullness thresholds, and that when a tote is full Chat is told the chute is unavailable during exchange.

steps.instruction: Identify that the tote is in the full state.
steps.expected_outcome: The tote is recognized as full rather than pretty full.
steps.validation_check: The slide defines the full threshold as the point where no more product can be diverted to the location until an exchange is done.
steps.visual_region_hint: Lower half of slide where the two thresholds and full-state definition are listed.

steps.instruction: Tell Chat that the chute is unavailable while the exchange is being done.
steps.expected_outcome: Packages do not come down the chute during the tote exchange.
steps.validation_check: The slide explicitly states that when a tote is full, Chat is told the chute is unavailable so that no packages come down while doing an exchange.
steps.visual_region_hint: Bottom bullet on the slide discussing full totes and chute unavailability.

warnings: The slide does not specify the exact interface or command used to tell Chat that the chute is unavailable.
The slide does not describe the exchange steps themselves.
retrieval_text: Operational action candidate from slide 12: when a tote is full, tell Chat that the chute is unavailable so no packages come down during exchange. The slide gives the condition and intended result, but not the exact UI or system steps.

title: Stage and complete a tote exchange when a tote is full or nearly full
preconditions: A tote is imminently full or completely full.
A no-tote AGV is designated to the tote's zone.
An AGV carrying an empty tote is available.
procedure_visual_summary: Slide describes AGV exchange staging around a full tote, including a zone-designated AGV positioning under racking, an empty-tote AGV approaching from behind under the sorter, the full-tote AGV moving into the aisle, and the empty tote being lowered into the rack location.

steps.instruction: Initiate exchange staging when a tote is imminently full or completely full.
steps.expected_outcome: The system enters an exchange scenario for the affected tote.
steps.validation_check: The slide states exchange staging can happen when a tote is imminently full or completely full.
steps.visual_region_hint: Upper text block describing exchange trigger conditions.

steps.instruction: Move the zone-designated AGV with no tote under the racking to position for a lift.
steps.expected_outcome: A no-tote AGV is positioned under the racking for the tote lift.
steps.validation_check: The AGV is described as moving under the racking to position for a lift.
steps.visual_region_hint: Middle text block beginning with 'An AGV with no tote...'.

steps.instruction: Lift the tote into position.
steps.expected_outcome: The tote is lifted and ready for the exchange movement.
steps.validation_check: The slide explicitly states the tote is lifted in position.
steps.visual_region_hint: Bullet line stating 'The tote is lifted in position'.

steps.instruction: Move an AGV carrying an empty tote under the sorter to the position behind that tote.
steps.expected_outcome: The replacement empty tote is staged behind the tote being exchanged.
steps.validation_check: The slide states the AGV with an empty tote travels under the sorter to the position behind that tote.
steps.visual_region_hint: Middle-lower text block beginning with 'An AGV with an empty tote...'.

steps.instruction: Move the AGV with the full tote out into the aisle.
steps.expected_outcome: The full tote clears the rack location.
steps.validation_check: The slide states the AGV with the full tote moves out into the aisle.
steps.visual_region_hint: Lower text block describing full-tote AGV movement into the aisle.

steps.instruction: Move the AGV with the empty tote into the rack location and lower its empty tote into the rack.
steps.expected_outcome: An empty tote is placed into the rack location vacated by the full tote.
steps.validation_check: The slide states the AGV with the empty tote moves into that rack location and lowers its empty tote into the rack.
steps.visual_region_hint: Lower text block describing empty-tote placement into the rack.

steps.instruction: Treat the AGV left with no tote as the AGV that will pick up the next full tote in that zone.
steps.expected_outcome: The no-tote AGV is reassigned as the next full-tote pickup vehicle for the zone.
steps.validation_check: The slide states this AGV with no tote becomes the AGV that will pick up the next full tote in that zone.
steps.visual_region_hint: Final bullet text at bottom of slide.

retrieval_text: Operational action for tote exchange staging: when a tote is imminently full or full, position a zone-designated no-tote AGV under the racking for lift, lift the tote, move an empty-tote AGV under the sorter behind the tote, move the full-tote AGV into the aisle, move the empty-tote AGV into the rack location and lower the empty tote, then use the resulting no-tote AGV as the next full-tote pickup AGV for that zone.

title: Start a tip and complete post-tip bag handling at the tipper
preconditions: The AGV has been sent to the tipper and reaches the tip location.
Software has informed the tipper that the AGV is staged for a tip.
The operator is at the tipper.
procedure_visual_summary: Slide describes the tipping flow from AGV arrival at the tipper through operator button press, automated tip, empty tote return, label application, bag placement on takeaway conveyor, and hanging the next empty bag.

steps.instruction: When the AGV is staged for a tip, push the button to start the tip.
steps.expected_outcome: The tipper starts the tip cycle.
steps.validation_check: The tipper begins clamping and tip motion after the button press.
steps.visual_region_hint: Text region describing operator action and tip start

steps.instruction: Allow the tipper to clamp the tote and perform the tip.
steps.expected_outcome: The tote is tipped by the machine.
steps.validation_check: The slide states the tipper clamps the tote and performs the tip.
steps.visual_region_hint: Text region describing tipper automated action

steps.instruction: After the tip, take the bag label and apply it to the bag.
steps.expected_outcome: The bag is labeled for downstream handling.
steps.validation_check: A bag label is present on the bag.
steps.visual_region_hint: Lower text region describing operator post-tip actions

steps.instruction: Place the bag on the takeaway conveyor.
steps.expected_outcome: The bag is transferred to the takeaway conveyor.
steps.validation_check: The bag is no longer at the tipper and is on the takeaway conveyor.
steps.visual_region_hint: Lower text region describing operator post-tip actions

steps.instruction: Hang the next empty bag for the next tip.
steps.expected_outcome: The tipper station is prepared for the next tipping cycle.
steps.validation_check: A new empty bag is hung and ready for the next tip.
steps.visual_region_hint: Lower text region describing operator post-tip actions

retrieval_text: Operator tipping action on slide 14: when the AGV is staged for a tip, the operator pushes a button to start the tip. The tipper clamps the tote and performs the tip. Afterward, the operator applies the bag label to the bag, places the bag on a takeaway conveyor, and hangs the next empty bag for the next tip.

title: Route AGV after full cycle completion based on system condition
preconditions: The full cycle is complete
The tipper informs software that the AGV is clear to move out
procedure_visual_summary: Slide states return behavior after cycle completion and lists destination outcomes for failed tip, low battery, and normal completion.

steps.instruction: Wait for the full cycle to complete and for the tipper to inform software that the AGV is clear to move out.
steps.expected_outcome: Software has clearance status indicating the AGV can depart.
steps.validation_check: Confirm slide-stated clearance condition exists: the tipper has informed software the AGV is clear to move out.
steps.visual_region_hint: Main body text under 'The Return'

steps.instruction: If the tip failed, route the AGV to Hospital.
steps.expected_outcome: The AGV is sent to Hospital following a failed tip.
steps.validation_check: Verify the AGV destination is Hospital when tip status is failed.
steps.visual_region_hint: Bullet list in lower half of slide

steps.instruction: If the battery level is low, route the AGV to the charger.
steps.expected_outcome: The AGV is sent to the charger when battery is low.
steps.validation_check: Verify the AGV destination is charger when battery level is low.
steps.visual_region_hint: Bullet list in lower half of slide

steps.instruction: If all conditions are good, return the AGV to the sorter entrance queue with an empty tote for its next exchange.
steps.expected_outcome: The AGV rejoins the sorter entrance queue carrying an empty tote for reuse.
steps.validation_check: Verify the AGV returns to the sorter entrance queue and has an empty tote for the next exchange.
steps.visual_region_hint: Bullet list in lower half of slide

warnings: The slide provides routing outcomes but does not specify who performs the routing action or the interface used.
Decision logic is limited to the conditions explicitly shown on the slide.
retrieval_text: Operational action candidate for AGV return routing after full cycle completion: once tipper informs software AGV is clear to move out, send failed tip to Hospital, low battery to charger, otherwise return AGV to sorter entrance queue with empty tote for next exchange.

title: Transition an AGV from one area to another via the common exit
preconditions: An AGV is in one defined layout area.
A destination area is different from the current area.
The layout uses distinct areas connected by a common entrance and exit.
procedure_visual_summary: Slide describes a layout rule: distinct areas are connected by a common entrance and exit, and AGVs must go to the exit before entering the next area. Areas listed are Hospital, SorterSide, Charger, and Tipper.

steps.instruction: When an AGV needs to move from its current area to a different area, route the AGV to the common exit first.
steps.expected_outcome: The AGV leaves the current area through the shared exit path.
steps.validation_check: Confirm the AGV goes to the exit before attempting entry into another area.
steps.visual_region_hint: Main bullet text describing area-to-area movement through the exit

steps.instruction: After reaching the exit, direct the AGV to enter the destination area.
steps.expected_outcome: The AGV enters the next area only after passing through the exit path.
steps.validation_check: Confirm area entry occurs only after the AGV has gone to the exit.
steps.visual_region_hint: Main bullet text stating the AGV must go to the exit before entering the next area

warnings: The slide provides a routing rule but not a detailed execution method or control interface.
No explicit user action, UI workflow, or command sequence is shown on the slide.
retrieval_text: Procedure candidate for AGV inter-area transition: move the AGV to the common exit first, then enter the destination area. Based directly on the slide rule that AGVs must go to the exit before entering the next area in the modular layout.
